concept: Sweat / The Skin's Water / To Sweat
description: Sweat: the skin's water in effort and heat — and the verb of producing it. The honest residue of 'riola' (labor).
sound_symbolism: 'si'+'we': the thinnest water word — skin-water in two light syllables.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'siwe mua menoa nai.' (Sweat is on the face.) It comes in labor: 'sui riola siwe shua.' (During labor, sweat comes.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Work that produces sweat is work at human scale — the body's own renewable engine, honored rather than engineered away.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Sweat was the measure of the day's work and the reason for the evening's washing — labor's plainest receipt.
